request.description_of_request:
The applicant is requesting a variance from the required rear yard setback in the R1-10 zoning district in order to construct a one-story, 240-square-foot addition to the existing single-family residence. Specifically, the City of Phoenix Zoning Ordinance requires a minimum rear yard setback of 25 feet for the subject property. Due to the irregular lot depth and the placement of the original home and pool (constructed in the early 1980s under different standards), strict enforcement of the current 25-foot rear setback would prevent any reasonably sized bedroom and bathroom addition.

The requested variance is to reduce the required rear yard setback from 25 feet to 15 feet along the western property line for a width of approximately 24 linear feet. The proposed addition will be integrated into the existing roofline and architectural style of the home, with stucco and tile roof materials matching the existing structure. The addition is intended to accommodate a ground-floor primary bedroom and accessible bathroom for the property owner’s elderly mother, who will be moving in with the family. This space will allow for barrier-free access, wider doorways, and a roll-in shower, which cannot reasonably be accommodated within the existing interior footprint without creating significant structural issues or removing the existing kitchen.

The hardship is created by the shallow and irregular shape of the lot (shorter depth than typical R1-10 lots in the subdivision) combined with the location of the existing pool and covered patio, which occupy the central portion of the rear yard. Placing the addition elsewhere on the lot would either require demolition of the existing pool (a primary recreation feature for the family and a significant financial investment) or would encroach into the required side yard setbacks and conflict with an existing underground sewer line. The requested 10-foot reduction in the rear setback is the minimum necessary to allow a functional, code-compliant bedroom and bathroom layout while preserving existing improvements and maintaining adequate open space and privacy for adjacent neighbors.

The addition will not be used as a separate dwelling unit or rental; it will remain part of the single-family residence and share all utilities and access. The applicant has spoken informally with the immediate rear neighbors at 1842 W. Willow Avenue, who indicated that they have no objection to the proposed addition, provided the height remains one story and no windows directly overlook their primary rear patio. In response, the design places windows higher on the wall facing the rear and utilizes frosted glass in the bathroom to maintain privacy. The applicant believes that granting this variance will not be detrimental to neighboring properties, will be in substantial conformity with the intent and purpose of the zoning ordinance, and is necessary to allow reasonable use of the property given its unique physical constraints.
request.existing_use_of_property:
The property is currently used as a single-family residence occupied by the applicant, their spouse, and two school-aged children. The existing home is a one-story, approximately 1,780-square-foot stucco house with a two-car garage, built around 1983 as part of the Desert Willow Estates subdivision. The house includes three bedrooms and two bathrooms, a combined living and dining area, and a modest kitchen that was partially remodeled in 2015.

The rear yard currently contains a rectangular in-ground swimming pool, a small grass area, a covered patio attached to the rear of the house, and a storage shed located near the northeast corner of the lot. The pool is located roughly in the center of the backyard, limiting buildable space to relatively narrow strips along the sides and rear of the existing structure. The side yards include a 5-foot-wide utility side yard on the east with the A/C condenser and a 10-foot-wide side yard on the west with a gate to the front driveway. The front yard is landscaped with low-water-use plants, including desert shrubs and a mature palo verde tree, in compliance with the subdivision’s CC&Rs.

There are no commercial activities being conducted at the property. The only non-residential use is a small home office used occasionally by the applicant for remote work, but this is entirely internal to the house and does not generate additional traffic, signage, or customer visits. The property is connected to city water and sewer and is served by standard residential utilities. There are no known code enforcement actions or zoning violations currently associated with the property.
request.existing_zoning_district: R1-10 Single-Family Residence District (City of Phoenix Zoning Ordinance)

property_information.general_location_include_nearest_city_to: The subject property is located on the south side of W Willow Avenue, approximately one block west of N 19th Avenue and just north of W Greenway Road, in northwest Phoenix, Maricopa County, Arizona.
property_information.legal_description: LOT 47, DESERT WILLOW ESTATES, ACCORDING TO BOOK 256 OF MAPS, PAGE 19, RECORDS OF MARICOPA COUNTY, ARIZONA.
